title: Collaboration Species

introduction.subtitle: How do you actually work with others? Five collaboration species and where you fit in a team.
introduction.description:
This maps what you genuinely do when work involves other people — not a wish-list of ideals. Mark your first and second instinct in each scenario. If several feel true, pick the one that most often wins when the pressure is real.

Invite three or more people to compare and see your team constellation.

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: Original instrument. This is not a validated psychometric assessment.

questions.text: You start a new venture. What is your ideal setup?

options.text: Work alone and deliver everything yourself.
One trusted co-founder in a deep partnership.
A small team of three to seven with clear roles.
A wider community gathered around the work.
Coach and enable others who actually do the building.

conceptHelp.title: Collaboration mode: You start a new venture. What is your ideal setup
conceptHelp.summary: A collaboration mode is the working arrangement where you tend to contribute best: solo creation, one deep partnership, small-team execution, community weaving, or enabling others. This question asks how that mode appears in "You start a new venture. What is your ideal setup?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Founders and builders often fail because the collaboration structure does not match their real operating style. The result gives vocabulary for designing work with less friction.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Choose the move that would feel naturally effective first, not the one that sounds most generous or impressive.
conceptHelp.example: The options contrast Work alone and deliver everything yourself. / One trusted co-founder in a deep partnership. / A small team of three to seven with clear roles.; your choice shows the collaboration pattern you would probably activate first.

questions.text: A big decision has to be made. You tend to...

options.text: Decide it yourself.
Decide it with your one trusted partner.
Bring it to the team and align.
Surface it to the wider community.
Coach the right person to make the call.

questions.text: You get a new idea. What do you do first?

options.text: Prototype it alone.
Call your one collaborator.
Assign roles and start together.
Open it to whoever is interested.
Find who should run with it.

questions.text: A hard deadline is approaching. You...

options.text: Lock in and ship it yourself.
Split the stretch with your partner.
Coordinate the team to hit it together.
Rally the community to help.
Clear the blockers for whoever is executing.

questions.text: Conflict breaks out in a group you are part of. You...

options.text: Step aside and keep working alone.
Work it out with the one person who matters.
Facilitate the team to resolve it.
Rebuild trust across the whole group.
Help the people in conflict grow through it.

questions.text: Your work style is most enlivened when...

options.text: You control the whole thing end to end.
You have one person you truly trust.
A tight crew has your back.
A network is building something together.
Someone you coached is winning.

questions.text: You are given a stretch project. You...

options.text: Scope it so you can own it solo.
Bring in your one partner.
Form a small team for it.
Open it to the community.
Hand it to someone ready to grow into it.

questions.text: Recognition matters most when it comes from...

options.text: The quality of the finished work itself.
Your one trusted partner.
Your team seeing you as reliable.
The community that benefits.
The people you helped succeed.

questions.text: When things go wrong, your instinct is to...

options.text: Fix it yourself.
Go to ground with your partner.
Huddle with the team.
Call the community for help.
Make sure the right person is unblocked.

questions.text: A year from now, you would feel most proud that...

options.text: You built something whole, alone.
With your partner, you built something neither could alone.
Your team did something it could not do without you.
A community can now sustain the work.
Several people have grown past where you could take them.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
